meaning: v.(使)精神振作,(使)精力恢复,更新
examples: There is a hypothesis that we sleep so that our brain can repair and refresh itself.
If technological diffusion and implementation develop faster, workers will have to constantly refresh their skills.
Just like Windows XP, Lindows initially sets the screen to a flickery, eye-busting 60Hz refresh rate.
Do you refresh Facebook fifteen times an hour to track the likes on your status?
